event_description: DOJ joins whistleblower lawsuit (filed 2011) against UHG/Optum subsidiary Ingenix for alleged Medicare risk adjustment fraud; case alleges 'hundreds of millions - likely billions' in overcharges by systematically boosting risk scores in Medicare Advantage plans; UHG one of largest MA carriers

Q1 2018 results: total medical membership 48.9M (+2.4M YoY); MA +330K in quarter (+11% YoY); operating income up 19% to $4.1B; MCR 81.4% (down 100bp due to health insurance tax deferral); Brian Thompson (CEO Medicare & Retirement) publicly projects 8% long-term MA growth rate

Cardinal Health sells 55% stake in NaviHealth to private equity firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice for $650M; NaviHealth valued at approximately $1.2B; manages post-acute care for 4.5M Medicare Advantage seniors and serves 9 of top 10 health systems; NaviHealth now PE-controlled with PE financial return expectations

FY2018 results: revenue $226B (+12% YoY); Optum crosses $100B revenue milestone first time; UHC membership +2.4M; MA membership 9.5M; operations income $17.3B (+14%); MCR 81.6%; all four business lines growing; CEO Wichmann projects half of Americans under value-based contracts within a decade

Q1 2019 results: total medical membership 48.9M; MA continues 11% YoY growth trajectory; commercial +845K; full-year 2019 EPS outlook raised; UHC CEO Steve Nelson highlights association health plan growth opportunity under Trump administration policies

FY2019 results: revenue ~$242B; UHC revenues $193.8B (+5.6%); MA +325K to 5.3M members at year-end; Medicare & Retirement revenues $83.3B (+10.3%); operations income $10.3B (+13.3%); cash flows $18.5B; share repurchases $5.5B; dividend growth 18.4% YoY

Baseline: UHC post-acute care prior authorization denial rate for skilled nursing facilities: 1.4%; this figure becomes the pre-NaviHealth baseline documented in Senate PSI report for comparison against 2022 post-deployment figures

COVID-19 pandemic declared; healthcare utilization drops sharply in Q2 2020 as elective procedures deferred; UHG medical care ratio declines as members pay premiums but avoid care; margins improve in insurance division during acute phase; COVID creates data distortion in post-acute utilization patterns that will inform AI training data

OptumHealth acquires NaviHealth for approximately $2.5B from PE firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice; stated rationale: combine NaviHealth post-acute clinical model with Optum community care to deliver value-based care for complex patients after hospital discharge; NaviHealth to remain standalone entity under OptumHealth with CEO Clay Richards retained; NaviHealth manages 4.5M MA seniors across 50 states

FY2020: UHC prior authorization denial rate for all post-acute care: 10.9% (Senate PSI report baseline figure); COVID year depresses baseline utilization and claim patterns; NaviHealth integration into Optum underway; MA membership growing; UHG revenues strong despite pandemic disruption

UHG announces proposed $13B acquisition of Change Healthcare - largest US healthcare payment clearinghouse processing approximately 15 billion claims per year; American Hospital Association and major hospital lobby immediately raise antitrust concerns about UHG gaining access to competitor insurance company data

David Wichmann retires as UHG CEO; Optum head Andrew Witty named new UHG CEO; first major leadership change in years; Witty comes from Optum (services/technology) rather than insurance side; signals continued emphasis on vertical integration of insurance and care delivery

Brian Thompson named CEO of UnitedHealthcare (full insurance division covering 50M+ members globally); previously ran UHC government programs including Medicare & Retirement - the division overseeing NaviHealth deployment; Thompson focuses on value-based care and continued MA growth; UHC contributes ~$200B of UHG revenue

FY2021: NaviHealth integration into OptumHealth continues; preparation underway for NaviHealth to take over management of UHC IE-SNP post-acute care authorizations; April 2022 deployment target set; MA membership at record levels with continued growth trajectory
source_name: Snopes UHC AI Denials Fact Check

event_description: DOJ and state AGs of New York and Minnesota file lawsuit to block Change Healthcare acquisition; allege merger would give UHG unfair access to competitor health insurer data; allege near-monopoly in claims editing (80%+ market share); judge allows trial to proceed; hospital lobby testifies against merger

NaviHealth begins managing post-acute care prior authorizations for UHC IE-SNP Medicare Advantage members - nH Predict AI algorithm now fully operational; algorithm compares individual patients against historical cohorts and generates Estimated Length of Stay predictions used in coverage decisions
source_name: Senate PSI Report / Snopes Fact Check

event_description: NaviHealth issues internal mandate to customer service representatives: 'IMPORTANT: Do NOT guide providers or give providers answers to the questions' regarding prior authorization; human guidance layer removed from provider support simultaneously with AI deployment rollout

Gary Bent case (approximate date): Gary Bent recovering from brain surgery for melanoma; NaviHealth care coordinator calls wife Gloria to set discharge date before clinical evaluation complete; coordinator recommends permanent wheelchair placement over attending neurosurgeon's intensive rehab recommendation; Gary Bent later dies; case becomes basis for Senate testimony

HHS Office of Inspector General publishes report on 2019 Medicare Advantage denials; finds private insurers used internally developed criteria - not Medicare's official coverage criteria - to delay or deny care to MA members; documents pre-NaviHealth pattern of non-compliant denials across industry

Federal court rules against DOJ challenge to Change Healthcare merger; Judge Nichols finds DOJ arguments rely on 'speculation rather than real-world evidence'; antitrust challenge fails; Change Healthcare ordered to divest ClaimsXten to TPG Capital for $2.2B as condition of approval

Change Healthcare merger closes at $7.8B; integrated into OptumInsight; UHG now controls largest US healthcare clearinghouse processing ~15B claims/year (~40% of all US healthcare claims); DOJ files notice of intent to appeal in November

UHC forms internal working group to apply machine learning to predict which post-acute care denial cases are likely to be appealed and which of those appeals are likely to be overturned; using data from denial and appeal history to optimize the denial pipeline

FY2022: Revenue $324B (+14%); profit $20.6B; MA post-acute denial rate 22.7% (up from 10.9% in 2020 - more than doubled in two years); SNF denial rate 12.6% (vs 1.4% in 2019 - 9x increase); expedited MA appeals filed 2020-2022 up 58%; nearly 150K appeals filed in 2022; record revenues concurrent with accelerating denial rates

STAT News investigation: Medicare Advantage AI driving denials to 'new heights'; NaviHealth nH Predict algorithm used to cut off post-acute care for seriously ill patients; clinicians pressured to follow algorithm predictions within 1% of projected days; staff report choosing 'between job performance and conscience'; NaviHealth externally calls tool 'a guidepost' while internally mandating strict adherence

CMS launches investigation into UnitedHealth following STAT News reporting; agency announces strengthened rules restricting insurer use of internal criteria (including algorithms) for coverage determinations; promises to increase MA denial audits; marks first federal regulatory action specifically targeting AI-driven MA denials

DOJ drops appeal of Change Healthcare merger; antitrust challenge formally abandoned; Change Healthcare fully absorbed into OptumInsight; UHG now controlling largest claims clearinghouse with no remaining federal challenge

Q1 2023 earnings: UHC revenues $70.1B (+17% YoY); total UHG revenue $91.9B (+15%); 2M new members vs Q1 2022 including 665K commercial; MA growing strongly; CEO Thompson tells analysts company sees 'plenty of room to grow in 2024'; full-year EPS guidance raised to $24.50-$25.00

Senate Finance Committee hearing on MA algorithm denials; Gloria Bent testifies about husband Gary denied post-acute care after brain surgery; attorney Christine Huberty testifies seniors face 'impossible system' against AI denials; senators warn MA insurers cannot use algorithms to override medically necessary care determinations

STAT News follow-up investigation: NaviHealth's own 2022 Optum-published paper shows it achieved savings of more than 20% per episode of care; Select Medical (original NaviHealth funder) publicly condemns company it once backed; providers nationwide report increase in denials tied directly to NaviHealth contract transition

30 Congressional representatives send letter to CMS urging oversight of AI and algorithmic tools in Medicare Advantage coverage decisions; led by Reps. Chu and Nadler; CMS has not yet required transparency in how AI tools are developed or validated; regulatory gap documented

Second STAT News investigation: UHG instructing frontline clinical staff to cut off patient payments in lockstep with nH Predict algorithm regardless of whether additional days are clinically justified; company issuing new denials immediately after patients win appeals; NaviHealth sets internal 2023 target to keep rehab stays within 1% of algorithm projection

Class action lawsuit filed (Lokken v. UHG et al.) in US District Court Minnesota: plaintiffs allege nH Predict has 90% error rate - 9 of 10 appealed denials overturned; company continues using system because only ~0.2% of policyholders appeal; elderly patients 'kicked out of care facilities nationwide' or 'forced to deplete family savings'

FY2023: Revenue $371.6B (+14.6%); operating income $32.4B (+13.8%); cash flows $29.1B; record revenues and profits during period of active lawsuit (Nov 2023) congressional scrutiny and ongoing denial rate controversy; profit margin stable despite escalating legal and regulatory pressure

ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware group gains initial access to Change Healthcare systems using compromised employee credentials on remote access portal lacking multi-factor authentication; attackers spend 9 days moving laterally through network and exfiltrating sensitive data before deploying ransomware on Feb 21

Brian Thompson sells approximately 31% of his UHG shares (~$15M profit) approximately 11 days before Wall Street Journal publishes story about DOJ antitrust investigation; Democratic senators later ask SEC to investigate potential insider trading

Change Healthcare ransomware deployed; systems taken offline; disrupts approximately 15 billion claims per year (~40% of all US healthcare claims); nationwide provider payment crisis begins; largest healthcare data breach in US history unfolds - eventually 190 million Americans affected; providers unable to submit claims for weeks to months

DOJ opens separate antitrust investigation into UHG; focuses on Optum's acquisition of physician practices (90K physicians - 1 in 10 US doctors); investigators ask whether UHC favors Optum providers in contracting; also investigates Medicare risk adjustment billing practices; UHG stock falls on news

Senators Warren and Markey write to SEC requesting investigation of UHG executive stock sales before DOJ antitrust investigation story broke; cite Thompson's $15M stock sale 11 days before WSJ story as potentially improper; UHG declines comment

CEO Andrew Witty testifies before Congress on Change Healthcare ransomware attack; confirms compromised system lacked MFA; says Change HC came in with 'older technologies'; UHG has paid $22M ransom; eventually 190M Americans' protected health information breached; House chair calls it 'a case study in crisis mismanagement'

52 bipartisan lawmakers send letter to CMS calling for prohibition on MA insurers using AI to deny care pending systematic review; UHG simultaneously rebrands NaviHealth to 'Optum Home & Community Care'; rebranding coincides with active lawsuit and congressional scrutiny; lawsuit specifically names NaviHealth

Senate Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations publishes report 'How Medicare Advantage Insurers Have Denied Patients Access to Post-Acute Care': documents UHC denial rate 10.9% (2020) to 22.7% (2022); SNF denials increased 9x (1.4% to 12.6%); 'multiple initiatives to automate process' documented during same period; calls for CMS audits when denial rates spike; UHC disputes findings

CEO Brian Thompson shot and killed outside NYC Hilton Midtown before scheduled investor meeting; shell casings found inscribed with words 'deny' 'defend' 'depose'; social media response reflects widespread public anger at health insurance system; Luigi Mangione later arrested and charged with murder; Tim Noel named successor CEO

FY2024: Revenue $400.3B (+8% YoY); profit $14.4B; UHC operating margin 5.2%; multiple ongoing pressures: class action proceeding; Change HC ransomware fallout ($2.5B total cost); DOJ antitrust probe active; Senate report public; MA utilization rising; 2025 sees sharp margin compression
